title: Probability, Counting, Expected Value, and Risk
description: Compare warranties, games, reliability claims, and scheduling risks with transparent assumptions. Learners cumulatively produce a practical risk and expected-value brief through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define outcomes and sample spaces before assigning probabilities.
Use complement, addition, and multiplication rules with explicit event relationships.
Interpret conditional probability as a changed reference group.
